food_name: Lemang (Bamboo Glutinous Rice Cooked in Coconut Milk)
region_of_origin: Malay Archipelago — Malaysia, Singapore, Indonesia, Brunei
food_subgroup: Glutinous rice cooked in coconut milk in bamboo tube

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for lemang. Closest parent is glutinous rice cooked in coconut milk. No FoodOn URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Glutinous rice cooked in coconut milk in bamboo tube (Malay lemang style)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00PD (rice-based preparations) used as closest parent; no specific lemang code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for lemang. Not assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of lemang is approximately 3 slices (150g), as commonly consumed alongside rendang or serunding during Hari Raya festivities.

nutrient_source.title: Lemang — IMR Malaysia MyFCD
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the IMR Malaysia MyFCD entry for Lemang. This is the primary approved regional source for Malaysian dishes. Values are accepted as verified.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Glutinous Rice (Pulut) — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for lemang in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 85 is derived from the closest analogous food: glutinous rice (pulut), which has one of the highest GI values among rice preparations due to its near-pure amylopectin structure and minimal resistant starch formation. The fat content from coconut milk provides a minor buffering effect but does not substantially lower the GI. Glycemic load per 150g serving calculated as: (85 × 45.75g carbohydrate) / 100 = 38.9, rounded to 38.6 per input source. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.guidance: A 150g serving of lemang contributes approximately 278mg sodium, which is moderate in isolation, but sodium load increases substantially when lemang is consumed alongside high-sodium accompaniments such as rendang, sambal, or serunding — patients with hypertension should monitor total meal sodium carefully and limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic index (~85) and very high glycemic load (~38.6 per 150g serving) due to near-pure amylopectin glutinous rice
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lemang has a very high GI (~85) driven by glutinous rice's near-pure amylopectin structure, and a 150g serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 38.6 — one of the highest among festive rice dishes — making it unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; if consumed at all during Hari Raya, portion should be limited to one small slice (~50g) and paired with high-fibre, low-GI foods to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very high saturated fat content (8.2g per 100g; 12.3g per 150g serving) from full-fat coconut milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lemang contains 8.2g saturated fat per 100g — predominantly from full-fat coconut milk — meaning a 150g serving delivers approximately 12.3g saturated fat, which exceeds the recommended daily saturated fat limit for cardiovascular risk reduction; individuals with cardiovascular disease or dyslipidaemia should treat lemang as an occasional festive food only and limit to one small serving per occasion.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Proinflammatory combination of very high saturated fat and very high glycaemic load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The combination of very high saturated fat (8.2g/100g from coconut milk) and very high glycaemic load creates a proinflammatory nutritional profile; patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit lemang to one small serving per festive occasion and consider substituting with ketupat, which has lower fat and a lower GI.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemang is a traditional Malay and Indonesian festive food that is central to Hari Raya Aidilfitri and Hari Raya Aidiladha celebrations across Malaysia, Singapore, Indonesia, and Brunei. It is prepared by soaking white glutinous rice (pulut) overnight, mixing it with full-fat coconut milk and salt, then pouring the mixture into hollowed bamboo tubes (buluh) lined with fresh banana leaves. The banana leaf lining prevents the rice from sticking to the bamboo and imparts a subtle herbal fragrance. The bamboo tubes are then leaned upright near glowing charcoal fires and rotated periodically over 3–5 hours to ensure even cooking. The result is a dense, fragrant, cylindrical log of rice that is sliced into rounds and served alongside rendang (spiced braised beef or chicken) and serunding (dry-fried spiced coconut). The texture is sticky, moist, and rich, with a distinctive smoky and woody aroma from the bamboo and charcoal. Nutritionally, lemang is one of the highest-calorie and highest-GI rice dishes in Malay cuisine: it provides 215 kcal per 100g, 9.5g fat (of which 8.2g is saturated fat from coconut milk), and 30.5g carbohydrate from glutinous rice. The glycemic index is approximately 85 (high), driven by glutinous rice's near-pure amylopectin structure, and a standard 150g serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 38.6 — extremely high for a single side dish. Lemang is vegetarian, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is not suitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease due to its very high GI and saturated fat content.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the IMR Malaysia MyFCD (Institute for Medical Research Malaysia), which is the primary approved regional source for Malaysian dishes. The GI value is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for glutinous rice (pulut), the primary ingredient, with a documented estimation basis and confidence of 0.9. All core macros are complete and internally consistent.
notes: Cholesterol is confirmed zero per IMR Malaysia MyFCD — lemang contains no animal-derived fat; coconut milk is plant-based and contains no dietary cholesterol. Vitamin A is confirmed zero per IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Vitamin B12 is confirmed zero as lemang contains no animal products. Vitamin D is confirmed zero as lemang contains no animal products or fortified ingredients. Trans fat is confirmed zero per IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat values are not reported by the IMR Malaysia MyFCD source and are set to -1. Water content is not reported by the source and is set to -1. The two portions in the input (100g and 150g) are preserved; the default serving used for glycemic load calculation is 150g (3 slices), which reflects typical festive consumption. FoodOn URI is null as no specific FoodOn concept exists for lemang; closest parent is glutinous rice cooked in coconut milk. SNOMED CT code is null as no specific concept exists. FoodEx2 code A00PD is used as the closest defensible parent code.
